subject: [PATCH v2 1/2] syscall_user_dispatch: Make it configurable in Kconfig

reviews.author: Matthew Wilcox
reviews.summary: Reviewer Matthew Wilcox expressed confusion about the naming convention used in the patch, specifically that 'IP' is being used for both Internet Protocol and Instruction Pointer, which he finds unclear.
reviews.raw_body:
I was very confused when I read this.  IP expands to Internet Protocol
long before it gets to Instruction Pointer in my brain.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch does not need to be changed because it was following a previous explanation from tglx, and thus no fix is planned.
reviews.raw_body:
Hah, fair, i was just going with tglx's [1] explanation.

https://lore.kernel.org/all/87a4s8m69c.ffs@fw13/

2026-07-04.patch_summary: This patch makes the Syscall User Dispatch feature configurable in Kconfig, allowing it to be enabled or disabled independently of CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL. The feature is used to intercept and redirect system calls outside an allowed IP address range to a userspace signal handler. By making it configurable, users can choose whether to enable this feature on their system.

reviews.author: Thomas Gleixner
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted a space/tab mismatch in the code and pointed out that the mechanism allows filtering either outside or inside an allowed IP address range, depending on the mode passed to the PRCTL
reviews.raw_body:
Space/TAB mismatch.

Also the mechanism allows to filter either outside an allowed range or
inside an allowed range depending on the mode which is handed to the PRCTL.

reviews.author: Randy Dunlap
reviews.summary: Reviewer Randy Dunlap simply echoed the original patch description, indicating a lack of specific feedback or concerns.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about inconsistent language in the patch and agreed to use more general terms.
reviews.raw_body:
hadn't noticed that feature actually got merged.

ack, will just go with the general language

2026-07-05.patch_summary: This patch makes the Syscall User Dispatch feature configurable in Kconfig, allowing it to be enabled or disabled independently of CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL. The feature is used to intercept and redirect system calls outside an allowed IP address range to a userspace signal handler. By making it configurable, users can choose whether to enable this feature on their system.
